# Readiness Checklist: STM Phase C-E

**Purpose**: Validate readiness and completeness of the Phase C management API, Phase D runtime consistency and operator tooling, and Phase E test realignment requirements before task generation and implementation review.
**Created**: 2026-03-23
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit.checklist` command based on the clarified feature spec, plan, supporting design artifacts, current OpenAPI drift, and constitution gates.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Are all Phase C workspace, session, and conversation management resources covered with explicit create, list, detail, update, and archive requirements where applicable, without leaving any CRUD or lifecycle gaps across the hierarchy? [Completeness, Spec §FR-C01, Spec §FR-C03, Spec §FR-C06]
- [ ] CHK002 Are ownership and parent-chain validation requirements specified consistently for workspace, session, and conversation operations, including the secure 403/404 behavior for out-of-hierarchy access? [Consistency, Spec §FR-C02, Spec §FR-C05, Spec §FR-C07]
- [ ] CHK003 Are the route-shape requirements complete for both nested and direct access patterns, including the required `/api/workspaces`, `/api/sessions/{session_id}`, and `/api/conversations/{conversation_id}` surfaces? [Completeness, Spec §FR-C13, Spec §FR-C14, Spec §FR-C15]
- [ ] CHK004 Are lifecycle requirements fully specified for `active`, `closed`, `summarized`, and `archived` states, including which mutations remain allowed and which become forbidden at each state boundary? [Clarity, Spec §FR-C04, Spec §FR-D15, Spec §Edge Cases]
- [ ] CHK005 Are archive-only semantics defined completely enough to prevent accidental hard-delete behavior, including cascade expectations from workspace and session parents to all descendants? [Completeness, Spec §FR-C09, Spec §FR-C10, Spec §SC-004]
- [ ] CHK006 Are cross-cutting management API requirements present for pagination metadata, GET idempotence, and error-envelope parity with chat, rather than being implied or left to implementation inference? [Completeness, Spec §FR-C08, Spec §FR-C11, Spec §FR-C12]
- [ ] CHK007 Are runtime consistency requirements complete for metadata auto-create, per-turn counter updates, token tracking, additive focused symbol updates, and coordinated checkpoint-plus-metadata writes? [Completeness, Spec §FR-D01, Spec §FR-D02, Spec §FR-D03, Spec §FR-D04, Spec §FR-D05]
- [ ] CHK008 Are reconciliation and migration requirements complete for orphan detection, checkpoint-metadata mismatch detection, JSON report output, dry-run, resumability, zero data loss, and mixed-traffic support during the migration window? [Completeness, Spec §FR-D06, Spec §FR-D07, Spec §FR-D08, Spec §FR-D11, Spec §FR-D13, Spec §FR-D14, Spec §FR-D15]
- [ ] CHK009 Are Phase E requirements complete for hierarchy, STM isolation, lifecycle enforcement, data consistency, legacy identity-model replacement, and operator-boundary regression coverage? [Completeness, Spec §FR-E01, Spec §FR-E02, Spec §FR-E03, Spec §FR-E04, Spec §FR-E05, Spec §FR-E06]

## Contract And OpenAPI Alignment

- [ ] CHK010 Does the plan explicitly require [docs/openapi.yaml](../../../docs/openapi.yaml) to be reconciled against the existing public API surface as well as the planned Phase C endpoints, rather than treating OpenAPI work as optional feature documentation? [Traceability, Plan §Existing Public API Surface, Constitution §Golden Development Rules.9]
- [ ] CHK011 Are the existing public REST endpoints that must remain represented in [docs/openapi.yaml](../../../docs/openapi.yaml) identified clearly enough for review, including `/api/health`, `/api/chat`, `/api/config`, `/api/models/openai*`, and `/api/users*`? [Completeness, Plan §Existing Public API Surface, Plan §Existing Public API and OpenAPI Reconciliation]
- [ ] CHK012 Is the documented chat contract drift between legacy `session_id` semantics and the Phase C-E `conversation_id` model called out explicitly, with a requirement to update OpenAPI request, response, and error examples where applicable? [Clarity, Spec §FR-D01, Spec §FR-D15, Plan §Existing Public API and OpenAPI Reconciliation, Gap]
- [ ] CHK013 Does the checklisted scope require an explicit disposition decision for the legacy `/api/sessions/{session_id}/conversation` documentation in [docs/openapi.yaml](../../../docs/openapi.yaml), including whether it is deprecated, replaced, or retained temporarily with migration notes? [Ambiguity, Plan §Existing Public API and OpenAPI Reconciliation, Gap]
- [ ] CHK014 Are management API route contracts specified with enough precision to translate directly into OpenAPI path items, including POST archive actions, nested parent-match rejection behavior, and required hierarchy identifiers in payloads? [Measurability, Spec §FR-C14, Spec §FR-C15, Spec §FR-C16, Spec §FR-C17, Contract §Management API]
- [ ] CHK015 Are response and error-shape requirements detailed enough that reviewers can determine whether [docs/openapi.yaml](../../../docs/openapi.yaml) matches the intended JSON envelope instead of relying on code inspection alone? [Acceptance Criteria, Spec §FR-C11, Contract §Common Rules]
- [ ] CHK016 Does the feature definition reflect the constitution rule that REST API changes are not checklist-complete unless OpenAPI updates are planned, reviewable, and compared against the actual registered public routes? [Consistency, Constitution §Golden Development Rules.9, Constitution §Spec-Kit REST API Gate]

## Cross-Artifact Alignment

- [ ] CHK017 Do the planning decisions in [research.md](../research.md) map cleanly into the operational and contract artifacts, with no unresolved contradictions on pagination, nested parent-match rejection, closed-versus-archived session behavior, summarized conversation semantics, reconciliation scheduling, migration window policy, operator-only execution, or coverage non-regression? [Traceability, Research §Decision 1, Research §Decision 2, Research §Decision 3, Research §Decision 4, Research §Decision 5, Research §Decision 6, Research §Decision 7, Research §Decision 8]
- [ ] CHK018 Does [data-model.md](../data-model.md) preserve the same hierarchy, identifiers, lifecycle states, and invariants as [spec.md](../spec.md) and [plan.md](../plan.md), without reintroducing legacy `session_id == thread_id` assumptions or weakening parent ownership rules? [Consistency, Spec §Key Entities, Plan §Coverage Matrix, Data Model §Core Entities, Data Model §Invariants]
- [ ] CHK019 Do [contracts/management-api.md](../contracts/management-api.md) and [contracts/operator-tooling.md](../contracts/operator-tooling.md) cover the same boundaries defined in the spec and plan, keeping public management routes under `/api` while preserving reconciliation and migration as operator-only tooling outside the public route surface? [Boundary, Spec §FR-C13, Spec §FR-E06, Plan §WS-C4, Plan §WS-D2, Plan §WS-D3, Contract §Operator Tooling]
- [ ] CHK020 Does [quickstart.md](../quickstart.md) exercise the critical flows promised by the spec and plan, including management API smoke tests, lifecycle behavior, runtime metadata consistency, reconciliation, migration, operator-boundary expectations, and regression coverage checks? [Coverage, Spec §Success Criteria, Plan §Phase Outputs, Quickstart §Goal]
- [ ] CHK021 Do the checklist artifacts themselves use the same terminology and canonical identifiers as the rest of the feature set, including `conversation_id`, `thread_id == conversation_id`, `summarized`, `closed`, `archived`, and the hierarchy `workspace -> session -> conversation -> thread`? [Consistency, Spec §Assumptions, Data Model §Scope, Constitution §Memory Architecture Boundaries]
- [ ] CHK022 Is there an explicit review step ensuring [quickstart.md](../quickstart.md), [contracts/management-api.md](../contracts/management-api.md), and [docs/openapi.yaml](../../../docs/openapi.yaml) stay mutually consistent as the canonical validation guide, route contract, and published REST API document? [Traceability, Plan §WS-C4, Plan §Existing Public API and OpenAPI Reconciliation, Constitution §Spec-Kit REST API Gate]

## Scenario Coverage And Edge Cases

- [ ] CHK023 Are the primary management scenarios complete across workspace, session, and conversation flows, including archive cascades, detail aggregates, and history-summary retrieval, with no missing acceptance path for any major scope? [Coverage, Spec §User Story 1, Spec §User Story 2, Spec §User Story 3, Spec §User Story 4]
- [ ] CHK024 Are alternate and exception flows defined for closed-session mutation rejection, conversation creation under closed or archived parents, repeated archive requests, zero-result pagination, and parent-path mismatch handling? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-C04, Spec §FR-C08, Spec §FR-C14, Spec §FR-C15]
- [ ] CHK025 Are runtime failure scenarios addressed clearly enough, especially metadata-write failure after chat delivery, archived-conversation chat rejection, and concurrent archive-versus-message races? [Coverage, Spec §Edge Cases, Spec §FR-D02, Spec §FR-D03, Spec §SC-015]
- [ ] CHK026 Are reconciliation edge cases covered, including ambiguous-parent anomalies, idempotent repeated scans, and machine-readable remediation guidance without implying auto-repair behavior? [Coverage, Spec §FR-D08, Spec §Edge Cases, Data Model §Reconciliation Report]
- [ ] CHK027 Are migration edge cases covered for dry-run with no legacy data, partially migrated datasets, interrupted-and-resumed execution, and legacy-request compatibility without reviving `session_id == thread_id` as a caller contract? [Coverage, Spec §FR-D11, Spec §FR-D13, Spec §FR-D15, Spec §Edge Cases, Research §Decision 6]

## Non-Functional And Boundary Requirements

- [ ] CHK028 Are performance requirements quantified for management API latency, metadata visibility timing, and reconciliation traffic impact so reviewers can tell whether acceptance criteria are objectively measurable? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005, Spec §SC-006, Spec §SC-008]
- [ ] CHK029 Are auditability and observability requirements specified distinctly for reconciliation reports versus reconciliation scan logs versus migration audit entries, instead of conflating output artifacts with operational logs? [Clarity, Spec §FR-D08, Spec §FR-D08a, Spec §FR-D12, Data Model §Reconciliation Scan Log Entry, Data Model §Migration Audit Entry]
- [ ] CHK030 Are operator-only security boundaries documented strongly enough to keep reconciliation and migration outside public `/api` and Socket.IO surfaces, with no ambiguity about whether admin HTTP endpoints are allowed in this phase? [Consistency, Spec §FR-E06, Contract §Operator Tooling, Research §Decision 7]
- [ ] CHK031 Are backward-compatibility expectations explicit for the migration window, including continued support for legacy stateless chat requests while preventing the deprecated `session_id == thread_id` identity model from re-entering the public contract? [Consistency, Spec §FR-D15, Spec §FR-E05, Research §Decision 6]
- [ ] CHK032 Is the coverage-baseline non-regression obligation defined clearly enough for later tasking and review, including the NFR-6.1.3 soft gate and the expectation that agent-core coverage must not regress during Phase E? [Acceptance Criteria, Plan §Constraints, Plan §Planning Decisions Resolved in Phase 0, Research §Decision 8]

## Dependencies, Assumptions, And Review Readiness

- [ ] CHK033 Are the dependencies between public management API delivery, runtime consistency work, operator tooling, and test realignment documented well enough that task generation can sequence shared contract work before parallel feature implementation? [Traceability, Plan §Sequencing and Parallelization, Plan §Workstreams]
- [ ] CHK034 Are the assumptions about extending the existing Flask blueprints, service factory, repositories, and script-based operator tooling documented explicitly enough to avoid accidental greenfield designs or public-surface expansion? [Assumption, Plan §Project Structure, Plan §Edge Cases, Assumptions, and Operator/Security Constraints]
- [ ] CHK035 Are the management API and operator-tooling contracts specific enough to generate reviewable tasks for contract updates, boundary enforcement, runtime drift handling, migration safety, and OpenAPI synchronization without requiring reviewers to infer missing work from prose? [Acceptance Criteria, Contract §Management API, Contract §Operator Tooling, Quickstart §Goal]
- [ ] CHK036 Does the feature documentation establish a review-ready trace from spec requirements to plan workstreams to research decisions, data-model invariants, contract artifacts, quickstart validation, and checklist gates, so unresolved ambiguities around OpenAPI drift or operator exposure are surfaced before implementation begins? [Traceability, Spec §Functional Requirements, Plan §Coverage Matrix, Research §Decision 1, Data Model §Invariants, Constitution §Supremacy]

## Notes

- This checklist is intentionally review-oriented: it tests whether the requirements, contracts, and planning artifacts are complete and internally consistent enough to drive implementation and task generation.
- OpenAPI synchronization is a hard readiness gate for this feature because Phase C changes the public REST surface and Phase D affects the documented chat contract.
